text: This video shows a subhepatic appendix formiformis which was noticed during the exploration in a patient whom laparoscopic cholecystectomy was performed with the diagnosis of cholecystitis. Simultaneous laparoscopic appendectomy is considered right after cholecystectomy due to the possibility that patient's previous right upper quadrant abdominal pain may also be related to this atypically localized appendix.
The surgeon grasps and elevates the appendix to expose the mesoappendix. The mesoappendix is then dissected using ligature, separating it from the appendix.

The surgeon ligates the arteria appendicularis using a plastic polymer endoclip and cuts it with ligature. The stump of the appendix is closed with two plastic polymer endoclips, and the appendix is transected with scissors. The appendix is then placed in a specimen bag and removed from the abdomen.
